# Overview [CURRENT] — package: overview — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

The agent framework bridges the Embabel agent platform to the Multi-Agent IDE's domain. It defines the agent type system, integrates the LLM runner, manages tool registration, and provides the skill system. While the execution pipeline itself is covered in the **agent-execution** view, this view covers the structural components that the pipeline consumes: how agents are typed, how tools are registered, how prompts reach the LLM, and how skills are loaded.

# Embabel Integration [CURRENT] — package: embabel-integration — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

References: 7 files tracked (package: embabel-integration) — see `$.sections["Embabel Integration"]` in .metadata/HEAD

The single active Embabel agent class is `WorkflowAgent` (inner class of `AgentInterfaces`), annotated with `@EmbabelComponent(scan = false)`. All workflow phases route through its `@Action` methods:

```java
@Action(canRerun = true)
public OrchestratorRouting coordinateWorkflow(OrchestratorRequest input, OperationContext context) { ... }

@Action(canRerun = true)
public DiscoveryOrchestratorRouting runDiscoveryOrchestrator(DiscoveryOrchestratorRequest input, ...) { ... }

// ... 20+ more @Action methods
```

Embabel's `@Action(canRerun = true, cost = N)` drives the execution graph. Actions take typed blackboard objects as input and produce typed routing objects. Embabel automatically advances based on which objects exist on the `Blackboard`. The `OperationContext` bridges the Embabel `AgentProcess` to the application's LLM and tool infrastructure.

`WorkflowAgent` also implements `StuckHandler`: when the agent process gets stuck, it inspects `BlackboardHistory` for repeated `ContextManagerRequest` entries and delegates to the context manager with a `REPLAN` code.

**EmbabelUtil**: provides `resolveOperationContext(AgentPlatform, ArtifactKey, agentName)` — walks the `ArtifactKey` parent chain through `AgentPlatform.getAgentProcess()` to find the live process. This is how topology tools and interrupts locate a live agent from just a session ID.

**ACP Chat Model Registration**: `AcpChatModel` (Kotlin, `@Component`) implements both Spring AI `ChatModel` and `StreamingChatModel`. It is injected into Embabel as the `acp-chat-model` LLM via `OperationContext.ai().withFirstAvailableLlmOf("acp-chat-model", encodedOptions)`. The model delegates to the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) SDK: it resolves runtime options via `AcpChatOptionsString`, looks up the provider definition from `AcpModelProperties`, creates or reuses an `AcpSessionManager.AcpSessionContext` (keyed by session routing key = sessionId + provider + options fingerprint), launches a stdio transport process, and sends the formatted prompt via `session.prompt(content)`. `AcpSessionManager` holds a `ConcurrentHashMap<Any, AcpSessionContext>` of live sessions; each `AcpSessionContext` carries the coroutine scope, transport, protocol, client, session, permission gate, and sandbox translation. Session identity flows through the `ArtifactKey` hierarchy: the chat model key is the root, and each message creates a child key.

# Agent Type System [CURRENT] — package: agent-type-system — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

References: 13 files tracked (package: agent-type-system) — see `$.sections["Agent Type System"]` in .metadata/HEAD

`AgentType` is an enum with 27+ roles:

```
ORCHESTRATOR, ORCHESTRATOR_COLLECTOR,
DISCOVERY_ORCHESTRATOR, DISCOVERY_AGENT, DISCOVERY_AGENT_DISPATCH, DISCOVERY_COLLECTOR,
PLANNING_ORCHESTRATOR, PLANNING_AGENT, PLANNING_AGENT_DISPATCH, PLANNING_COLLECTOR,
TICKET_ORCHESTRATOR, TICKET_AGENT, TICKET_AGENT_DISPATCH, TICKET_COLLECTOR,
COMMIT_AGENT, MERGE_CONFLICT_AGENT,
AI_FILTER, AI_PROPAGATOR, AI_TRANSFORMER,
REVIEW_AGENT, REVIEW_RESOLUTION_AGENT, MERGER_AGENT,
CONTEXT_MANAGER, AGENT_CALL, CONTROLLER_CALL, CONTROLLER_RESPONSE, ALL
```

Each `AgentType` maps to an `AgentActionMetadata<T, U, V>` record:

```java
record AgentActionMetadata<T extends AgentRequest, U extends AgentRouting, V extends AgentResult>(
    AgentType agentType, String agentName, String actionName, String methodName,
    String template, Class<T> requestType, Class<U> routingType, Class<V> resultType,
    ErrorTemplates errorTemplates)
```

The full registry (`AgentMetadata.ALL`) enumerates every action. Lookups via `findByAction(agentName, actionName, methodName)` drive the entire execution pipeline. The `AgentType` also governs tool and prompt contributor filtering.

# Tool System [CURRENT] — package: tool-system — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

References: 13 files tracked (package: tool-system) — see `$.sections["Tool System"]` in .metadata/HEAD

# ToolAbstraction: The Unified Wrapper [CURRENT] — package: toolabstraction-the-unified-wrapper — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

`ToolAbstraction` is a sealed interface bridging Spring AI and Embabel tool models:

```
ToolAbstraction (sealed)
 ├─ SpringToolCallback          (Spring AI ToolCallback)
 ├─ SpringToolCallbackProvider  (Spring AI provider)
 ├─ EmbabelTool / EmbabelToolObject / EmbabelToolGroup  (Embabel variants)
 ├─ ToolGroupStrings            (string-keyed tool groups)
 └─ SkillReference              (prompt contributor, NOT callable — see Skills below)
```

`ToolContext` is an immutable record holding `List<ToolAbstraction>`. It passes through the decorator chain and is applied in `DefaultLlmRunner.applyToolContext()` via exhaustive switch.

# Three Tool Carrier Beans [CURRENT] — package: three-tool-carrier-beans — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

**AgentTopologyTools** (always active):
- `list_agents` — discovers callable vs. topology-prohibited agents
- `call_agent` — routes P2P message through full decorator pipeline to another agent's LLM session
- `call_controller` — suspends agent and blocks on human interrupt

**ContextManagerTools** (always active, exposed to context manager action):
- `traceBlackboardHistory`, `listBlackboardHistory`, `searchBlackboardHistory` — history exploration
- `addNoteToBlackboardHistory` — annotate entries with tags and notes

**AgentTools** (UI tools — currently disabled/commented out):
- `emitGuiEvent`, `retrieveGui`, `submitGuiFeedback`, `performUiDiff`

All use `@SetFromHeader(MCP_SESSION_HEADER)` for automatic session ID injection.

# EmbabelToolObjectRegistry [CURRENT] — package: embabeltoolobjectregistry — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

Handles lazy-loaded `ToolObject` lists (typically from MCP servers). Uses `computeIfPresent` for lock-striping and a 5-attempt retry with 3-second backoff to tolerate MCP server startup delays.

# LLM Runner: Template to ACP Call [CURRENT] — package: llm-runner — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

References: 5 files tracked (package: llm-runner) — see `$.sections["LLM Runner: Template to ACP Call"]` in .metadata/HEAD

`DefaultLlmRunner` (implements `LlmRunner`) is the bridge between the Embabel execution pipeline and the ACP transport layer. The full call chain:

```
DefaultLlmRunner.runWithTemplate(templateName, promptContext, model, toolContext, responseClass, context)
 |
 ├─ 1. resolveEncodedAcpOptions(promptContext)
 |    ├─ If promptContext.modelName() is already encoded (AcpChatOptionsString.looksLikeEncodedModel) → use as-is
 |    └─ Else resolve via LlmModelSelectionProperties.resolve(agentType, templateName):
 |         Priority: byTemplate[templateName] > byAgentType[agentType] > defaultModel
 |         Returns LlmSelection(model, providerWireValue)
 |    └─ AcpChatOptionsString.create(chatId, model, provider, runtimeOptions).encodeModel(objectMapper)
 |         → Base64-encoded JSON: {sessionArtifactKey, requestedModel, requestedProvider, options}
 |
 ├─ 2. context.ai().withFirstAvailableLlmOf("acp-chat-model", encodedOptions)
 |    → Embabel resolves the registered ChatModel bean with the encoded options as the model identifier
 |    → AcpChatModel.call(Prompt) receives the encoded options via prompt.options.model
 |
 ├─ 3. promptContributorService.getContributors(promptContext) → ContextualPromptElement[]
 |    .withPromptElements(contributors) — inject all prompt contributors
 |
 ├─ 4. applyToolContext() — exhaustive switch over ToolAbstraction variants:
 |    SpringToolCallback      → .withToolObject(new ToolObject(cb))
 |    SpringToolCallbackProvider → iterates callbacks, each → .withToolObject()
 |    EmbabelTool             → .withTool(tool)
 |    EmbabelToolObject       → .withToolObject(to)
 |    EmbabelToolGroup        → .withToolGroup(tg)
 |    EmbabelToolGroupRequirement → .withToolGroup(requirement)
 |    ToolGroupStrings        → .withToolGroups(groups)
 |    SkillReference          → no-op (skills go through prompt contributors)
 |    Also always merges AskUserQuestionToolAdapter as a baseline tool.
 |
 ├─ 5. LlmCallDecorator chain (PromptHealthCheckLlmCallDecorator, ArtifactEmissionLlmCallDecorator)
 |
 └─ 6. .creating(responseClass).fromTemplate(templateName, model)
      → Embabel renders Jinja template with model variables
      → Sends assembled prompt to AcpChatModel
      → AcpChatModel decodes options via AcpChatOptionsString.fromEncodedModel()
      → Resolves provider from AcpModelProperties (defaultProvider + providers map)
      → Creates/reuses AcpSessionContext via AcpSessionManager
      → Formats prompt, sends via ACP session.prompt(), collects generations
      → Returns structured response parsed from LLM output
```

# Skill System [CURRENT] — package: skill-system — rendered: 2026-04-13T22:04:16Z

References: 2 files tracked (package: skill-system) — see `$.sections["Skill System"]` in .metadata/HEAD

Skills are **prompt-side injections**, not callable tools. The loading process:

```
SkillProperties (application.yml: skills.localSkills map)
 → SkillFinder.findSkillDecorator(name)
   → SkillLoader.loadSkill(name, description) → Embabel Skills object
   → .withLocalSkill(skillPath) for each configured path
   → SkillDecorator wrapper

SkillDecorator → ToolAbstraction.SkillReference
 → DefaultLlmRunner sees SkillReference → no-op (not a tool call)
 → Instead, skill content flows as ContextualPromptElement prompt contributors
   → injected into the prompt text as skill documentation/instructions
```
